drivers.label: Revenue growth (Yr 1–10)
drivers.comment: The earlier 8% opener lagged a full year of realized-plus-expected growth. Base now opens at ~15% (FY26 consensus), ~9% (FY27), ~7.6% (FY28), then fades to 4% by Yr 10. The base-year revenue stays anchored to reported FY2025 ($416.2B) with growth applied on top, so there is no double-count with the forward figure. Bull runs a sustained AI/Services supercycle (~16% → 5%); Bear stays skeptical (~6% → 2%).

drivers.label: EBIT margin (Yr 1–10)
drivers.comment: Q1 FY2026 operating margin already ran ~35% and blended gross margin ~49%, with Services at ~77% gross margin versus ~39% for products. As Services (>25% of revenue, growing double-digits) climbs, the blended operating margin should too. Base expands toward 38%; Bull toward 43%. Higher than the prior read and defensible on the trajectory, though the Bull ceiling is at the aggressive end.

drivers.label: Capex (Yr 1–10, % rev)
drivers.comment: ~3% of revenue — Apple is asset-light and outsources manufacturing, a deliberate contrast to the AI-infra names (MSFT/GOOGL at 15–28%). Not a swing factor; the model barely moves on capex.

drivers.label: D&A (% of revenue)
drivers.comment: Small and stable (~2.8% of revenue), consistent with the light asset base. Not a swing factor.

drivers.label: Net working capital (% of revenue)
drivers.comment: Apple famously runs *negative* working capital — payables and deferred revenue exceed receivables and inventory — so incremental growth is partly self-funding (a source of cash). Modeled from a small drag in Bear (+1%) to a small source in Bull (−1%). A real advantage over most peers.

drivers.label: Net cash (cash + securities − debt)
drivers.comment: Now uses Q2 FY2026 figures: cash + marketable securities ~$146.7B less total debt ~$84.7B. The earlier $78.3B debt captured only non-current term debt and omitted current maturities + commercial paper; the earlier $156.6B cash was the stale FY2024 total. Net cash is Apple's stated ~$62B, down from the ~$78B previously implied — so this correction trims ~$1/share. A credibility fix, not a gap-closer.

drivers.label: Tax rate
drivers.comment: The prior 21% used the US *statutory* federal rate; Apple's *effective* rate is far lower — 15.6% in FY2025 and ~17% TTM/guided (FY2024's 24.1% was distorted by the one-time ~$10.2B EU State Aid charge and is not representative). Set to a normalized 16%. This lifts NOPAT/FCF by ~6% across every scenario — the single highest-value, least-controversial correction, and it should never have been at statutory.

drivers.label: Risk-free rate
drivers.comment: ≈ the 10-year Treasury yield. Bumped to ~4.6%, matching mid-2026 levels (a touch above the prior 4.4–4.5%).

drivers.label: Equity risk premium
drivers.comment: 4.0–4.3% ≈ Damodaran's implied ERP of 4.23% (2026). This is where current market conditions legitimately enter the model.

drivers.label: Beta
drivers.comment: Lowered to ~0.95–1.10 (third-party estimates run 0.83–1.10). Apple's mean-reverting, quality profile argues for the lower half; Bull uses 0.95.

drivers.label: WACC (derived)
drivers.comment: CAPM build-up nets to ~8.2–9.2% across scenarios. The sell-side 'quality compounder' camp applies a sub-8% (even ~7%) discount rate to Apple, which is the biggest single lever separating a fundamentals DCF from the market. We deliberately keep the Base case at a CAPM-derived ~8.9% rather than importing that concession — so the model stays a conservative anchor and does not simply assume the re-rating is permanent.

drivers.label: Terminal growth
drivers.verdict: Base OK · Bull at the ceiling
drivers.comment: Terminal g should not exceed the ~4% risk-free / long-run nominal GDP. Bull's 3.5% sits right at that edge — appropriate only if you truly believe the AI/Services franchise still out-grows the economy in perpetuity.

drivers.label: Scenario probability
drivers.verdict: Corrected scenarios now bracket the market
drivers.comment: With the factual fixes in (16% effective tax, near-term growth at Street consensus, Services-led margin expansion), the scenarios finally span the tape: Base ~$220, Bull ~$334, weighted ~$253 at Bear 15 / Base 45 / Bull 40. Two reads worth noting. First, Base ~$220 lands almost exactly on Apple's own 10-year-average P/E fair value (~25× forward EPS) — a good independent sanity check. Second, the market now sits between our Base and Bull (roughly a 70%-Bull weighting), no longer beyond the model's reach — so the earlier 'weights can't get there' ceiling was really a symptom of the wrong tax rate and stale growth, not the weights. The remaining distance from Base to price (~$95, ~30%) is multiple re-rating above Apple's decade norm — the AI/Services quality bid plus the ~2.4%/yr buyback — which a fundamentals DCF can only reproduce by conceding a sub-8% discount rate we chose not to assume.
